Name: Miami
goals:
To work on for the next step:

Mental / decision making racing:

- More comms when we have unstable breeze before approaching the next leg using the fleet, marks to set the plan. Wind angle, pressure, marks
And as we go on through the plan, rechecking that what we picked is good. Ian making questions to Nev to get into the planing strategy

- Intensity on decisions till the finish
Don’t think it’s done with only one decision made well and then only focus in performance.
Ian drives the boat and pushes Nev to keep head out to be 100% sure we are on the wright path

- In final days with uncertain  wind/plan, we should take a look where our rivals are staring at
Make sure we are not risking up too much by doing something completely different and put too many points in the game if doesn’t go well

Drills to practice in all conditions:
- Speed downwind / lay lines (tight lay lines UW under 9knts)
- Moño at gates (kiwi drop immediately tack) 
- Moño top mark
- Long long starting line starting at the middle (without transit)(coms of wind shift and changes to anticipate when the shoot will be)
- Manouvres with boundaries


Planing condition:
- Faster turn in tacks with flat water
- Develop Nevin’s way to hook himself out of gybes with the hand of the tiller
- Straight line trying Chinese mode (full full hike from toes to hair) want a photo!

Offshore breeze (any wind intensity)
- Short course 100% head out allll the time (don’t care performance)

Light
- Double gybe light breeze. Gybe, pump and go again (learn not to cross too much the boom in the middle to pass battens) grabbing boom from below
- In tight lanes, fast to get in when WW heel appears to avoid loosing side ways
- Accelerations (3 pumps and go)(how Ian will go, under lowers, horse step, 2nd or 3rd skul?)
- Go in circles around the rib keeping flow in every move

Above 20knts:
- Laps
